test_cases.test_case: Successful login redirects to Dashboard
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available; network connectivity to the authentication backend.
steps: 1. Select 'default' from the Tenant dropdown
2. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
3. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
4. (Optional) Check the Remember me checkbox
5. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: The Dashboard page is displayed; the main Dashboard heading and the top navigation (Institution, Accounting, Reports, Admin) are visible

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Forgot Password page via link
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available; network connectivity to the authentication backend.
steps: 1. Click the 'Forgot Password?' link below the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: The Forgot Password page is displayed showing its page title and an input to enter the account email or username

test_cases.test_case: Login button becomes enabled only after required fields are filled
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available; network connectivity to the authentication backend.
steps: 1. Select 'default' from the Tenant dropdown
2. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
3. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
4. Observe the Login button state
test_cases.expected_result: The Login button becomes enabled and is clickable after Tenant, Username and Password are filled

test_cases.test_case: Submit with ALL required fields empty
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available; network connectivity to the authentication backend.
steps: 1. Ensure Tenant selection is empty (clear the Tenant dropdown)
2. Leave the Username field blank
3. Leave the Password field blank
4. Click the Login button (or attempt to submit the form)
test_cases.expected_result: Login does not proceed; the Login button remains disabled / form does not submit. Inline validation errors are shown on the Tenant, Username and Password fields indicating they are required; the user remains on the Login page.

test_cases.test_case: Tenant left blank (dropdown) while other fields filled
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available.
steps: 1. Clear the Tenant dropdown so it is empty
2. Enter <a username> in the Username field
3. Enter <a password> in the Password field
4. Attempt to click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login is blocked; an inline validation error is shown on the Tenant field indicating it is required. The Login button remains disabled or the form does not submit; the user remains on the Login page.

test_cases.test_case: Username left blank (text) while other fields filled
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available.
steps: 1. Select a Tenant in the Tenant dropdown
2. Leave the Username field blank
3. Enter <a password> in the Password field
4. Attempt to click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login is blocked; an inline validation error is shown on the Username field indicating it is required. The Login button remains disabled or the form does not submit; the user remains on the Login page.

test_cases.test_case: Password left blank (password) while other fields filled
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available.
steps: 1. Select a Tenant in the Tenant dropdown
2. Enter <a username> in the Username field
3. Leave the Password field blank
4. Attempt to click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login is blocked; an inline validation error is shown on the Password field indicating it is required. The Login button remains disabled or the form does not submit; the user remains on the Login page.

test_cases.test_case: Submit with filled fields but invalid credentials
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available; network connectivity to the authentication backend.
steps: 1. Select a Tenant in the Tenant dropdown
2. Enter <an invalid username> in the Username field
3. Enter <an invalid password> in the Password field
4. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login does not succeed; a visible error message indicating invalid credentials is displayed (as specified by the system). The user remains on the Login page and is not redirected to the Dashboard.

test_cases.test_case: Username containing only whitespace enables Login button but authentication fails
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available
A valid account exists for the selected tenant with a non-whitespace username and a known password
steps: 1. Ensure the Tenant dropdown is set (use default or select a tenant)
2. Enter only whitespace characters into the Username field
3. Enter the valid account Password into the Password field
4. Observe the Login button state
5. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login is blocked: the Login button becomes enabled but clicking Login shows the invalid credentials error message inline

test_cases.test_case: Leading/trailing whitespace around Username is trimmed before authentication
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available
A valid account exists for the selected tenant with Username = <valid username> and Password = <valid password>
steps: 1. Ensure the Tenant dropdown is set (use default or select the tenant that owns the valid account)
2. Enter the valid username with additional leading and trailing whitespace into the Username field
3. Enter the exact valid password (no extra whitespace) into the Password field
4. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login succeeds: user is redirected to the Dashboard (visible Dashboard page) indicating the system trimmed the Username whitespace before authentication

test_cases.test_case: Extremely long Username and Password inputs (200+ characters)
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available
Tenant is selected or defaults to the default tenant
steps: 1. Ensure the Tenant dropdown is set (use default or select a tenant)
2. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) into the Username field
3. Enter a very long string (200+ characters) into the Password field
4. Observe the Login button state
5. Click the Login button
test_cases.expected_result: Login is blocked / error shown: the UI displays an inline validation message adjacent to the Username or Password field or the invalid credentials error after submit (visible error shown on the login form)

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Login with valid credentials
preconditions: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available
A valid account exists for the selected tenant with Username = <valid username> and Password = <valid password>
steps: 1. Ensure the Tenant dropdown is set (use default or select the tenant that owns the valid account)
2. Enter the valid username into the Username field
3. Enter the valid password into the Password field
4. Rapidly click the Login button twice in immediate succession
test_cases.expected_result: Login succeeds: user is redirected to the Dashboard once (Dashboard page appears) and no duplicate error messages or duplicate login forms are displayed

test_cases.test_case: Search recent system activity filters the Recent Activities list
preconditions: Authenticated user session (successful login) with application context loaded
steps: 1. Navigate to the Home Page (open the application's landing view)
2. Enter <search term> in the Search Activity input
3. Submit the search (press Enter or click the search control)
test_cases.expected_result: Recent Activities list displays only entries matching <search term>; unrelated activities are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Clicking Dashboard opens the Dashboard page
preconditions: Authenticated user session (successful login) with application context loaded
steps: 1. Navigate to the Home Page (open the application's landing view)
2. Click the Dashboard button
test_cases.expected_result: The Dashboard page opens; the page heading 'Dashboard' is visible

test_cases.test_case: Welcome card and system version information are visible on Home Page
preconditions: Authenticated user session (successful login) with application context loaded
steps: 1. Navigate to the Home Page (open the application's landing view)
2. Observe the main content area and the bottom of the page
test_cases.expected_result: The welcome card displays the text "Welcome, mifos!"; system version information for Mifos and Fineract is visible at the bottom of the page

test_cases.test_case: Access Home Page while unauthenticated (requires authenticated session)
preconditions: User is not authenticated
steps: 1. In a new browser session with no authentication, navigate to the Home Page URL
2. Observe the resulting page
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked due to missing authenticated session: browser is redirected to the Login page (login prompt displayed); Home Page content (welcome card and Search Activity input) is not visible

test_cases.test_case: Click Dashboard button when application context is not loaded (precondition failure)
preconditions: Authenticated user session
No application context loaded (no Institution selected)
steps: 1. Log in as a valid user
2. Ensure no application context (Institution) is selected for the session
3. Navigate to the Home Page
4. Click the Dashboard button
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked due to missing application context: click does not navigate to the Dashboard; user remains on the Home Page; the Dashboard button is disabled or an inline blocking indicator is shown near the Dashboard control indicating application context is required

test_cases.test_case: Directly navigate to Dashboard URL when application context is not loaded
preconditions: Authenticated user session
No application context loaded (no Institution selected)
steps: 1. Log in as a valid user
2. Ensure no application context (Institution) is selected for the session
3. In the browser address bar, navigate directly to the Dashboard URL
4. Observe the resulting page
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked due to missing application context: the Dashboard view is not displayed; the user is either redirected back to the Home Page or shown a blocking notice indicating application context is required; Home Page content remains visible

test_cases.test_case: Search input accepts very long string (200+ characters)
preconditions: Authenticated user session; Home Page is visible with Search_Activity input
steps: 1. Focus the Search_Activity input
2. Type a string of 200+ characters into the Search_Activity input
3. Press Enter (submit the search)
test_cases.expected_result: Search executes successfully; the Search_Activity input displays the full 200+ character string and the Recent Activity area updates to show filtered results or a visible 'no results' / empty-state message

test_cases.test_case: Search input with special characters and emoji
preconditions: Authenticated user session; Home Page is visible with Search_Activity input
steps: 1. Focus the Search_Activity input
2. Type a string containing special characters and emoji into the Search_Activity input
3. Click the search control or press Enter to submit
test_cases.expected_result: Search executes successfully; the Search_Activity input displays the exact special characters and emoji entered and the Recent Activity area updates to reflect the search (showing matching items or a visible 'no results' message)

test_cases.test_case: Search input handles leading and trailing whitespace
preconditions: Authenticated user session; Home Page is visible with Search_Activity input
steps: 1. Focus the Search_Activity input
2. Type a search term with leading and trailing whitespace into the Search_Activity input
3. Press Enter (submit the search)
test_cases.expected_result: Search executes successfully; the Search_Activity input displays the entered value with leading and trailing whitespace trimmed and the Recent Activity area updates to show filtered results or a visible 'no results' message

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Dashboard button
preconditions: Authenticated user session; Home Page is visible with Dashboard button
steps: 1. Click the Dashboard button
2. Immediately click the Dashboard button again within a short interval (e.g., two clicks within 300ms)
test_cases.expected_result: Single navigation to the Dashboard occurs (action succeeds); the Dashboard view is displayed and no duplicate navigations or duplicate visible dashboard pages are produced

test_cases.test_case: Open Dashboard and view Client Trends chart and summary cards
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Authenticated user on the Home Page with an office context available (selected or default)
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' button on the Home page
test_cases.expected_result: Dashboard page is displayed; the 'Client Trends' chart is visible with legends for 'New Clients' and 'Closed Clients'; two summary cards titled 'Amount Pending / Disbursed' and 'Amount Collected' are visible for the selected office

test_cases.test_case: Use Search Activity to filter dashboard activities
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Authenticated user on the Home Page with an office context available (selected or default)
Dashboard page is open
steps: 1. Focus the 'Search Activity' field at the top of the Dashboard page
2. Enter <search term> in the 'Search Activity' field
3. Press Enter to execute the search
test_cases.expected_result: Dashboard updates to show only activities matching <search term>; the activity view and any related chart or list reflect the filtered set and unrelated activities are no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Dashboard summary cards display 'No Data' when office has no financial information
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Authenticated user on the Home Page with an office context available (selected or default)
Selected office has no financial records for the summary period
steps: 1. Click the 'Dashboard' button on the Home page
test_cases.expected_result: Both summary cards titled 'Amount Pending / Disbursed' and 'Amount Collected' display the text 'No Data' for the selected office; the 'Client Trends' chart is still visible (may show empty or zeroed series)

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user attempts to open Dashboard from Home page
preconditions: User is not authenticated
Home Page is reachable
steps: 1. Open the Home Page as an unauthenticated user
2. Click the Dashboard button/link in the top navigation
test_cases.expected_result: User is not taken to the Dashboard; the application redirects to the authentication/login screen or shows a login prompt. The Dashboard page is not displayed and Client Trends chart and summary cards are not visible.

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated user with no office context attempts to open Dashboard
preconditions: User is authenticated
No office context is selected or available for the user
steps: 1. Sign in as an authenticated user who has no office context selected or assigned
2. From the Home Page, click the Dashboard button/link
test_cases.expected_result: Dashboard page does not load; the UI displays a visible error banner or inline message indicating an office context is required (office must be selected) and blocking access. The Client Trends chart and the summary cards (Amount Pending / Disbursed and Amount Collected) are not displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Very long search query is entered into Search Activity
preconditions: Authenticated user on the Home Page with an office context available
Dashboard page is opened
steps: 1. Focus the Search Activity field
2. Enter a very long search string (significantly longer than typical display width)
3. Press Enter or click the Search action
test_cases.expected_result: Search Activity input retains and displays the full entered string (no visible truncation in the input) and no inline validation error is shown for length

test_cases.test_case: Search query containing special characters and emoji
preconditions: Authenticated user on the Home Page with an office context available
Dashboard page is opened
steps: 1. Focus the Search Activity field
2. Enter a search string composed primarily of special characters and emoji
3. Press Enter or click the Search action
test_cases.expected_result: Search Activity input displays the exact entered special characters and emoji; no inline validation error is shown and the UI does not show an error state for the query

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in search term is handled
preconditions: Authenticated user on the Home Page with an office context available
Dashboard page is opened
steps: 1. Focus the Search Activity field
2. Enter a search term that includes leading and trailing whitespace
3. Press Enter or click the Search action
test_cases.expected_result: After search executes, the Search Activity input displays the value without leading or trailing whitespace (visible trimmed value) and no inline validation error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Selected office has no available data — chart and summary cards show 'No Data'
preconditions: Authenticated user on the Home Page with an office context available
The selected office has no client growth or financial data available
steps: 1. Click the Dashboard button from the Home Page
test_cases.expected_result: Client Trends chart area displays a visible 'No Data' indicator and both summary cards ('Amount Pending / Disbursed' and 'Amount Collected') each display a visible 'No Data' label

test_cases.test_case: Open the global search input via top-bar icon
preconditions: User is logged in with access to the application (and permission to view Clients/Groups/Loans/Savings) and is on any in-app page.
steps: 1. Click the search icon in the top toolbar.
test_cases.expected_result: The search input opens in the top toolbar and is focused; the text caret is visible inside the input.

test_cases.test_case: Perform a search that returns grouped results across Clients, Groups, Loans and Savings (partial & case-insensitive match)
preconditions: User is logged in with access to the application (and permission to view Clients/Groups/Loans/Savings) and is on any in-app page.
There exist entities in Clients, Groups, Loans, and Savings whose display name or identifier include variations of <search term> (including different letter casing and substrings).
steps: 1. Click the search icon in the top toolbar to open the search input.
2. Enter <search term> in the search input (use a partial substring and different letter case than stored values).
3. Observe the results dropdown.
test_cases.expected_result: A results dropdown appears grouped by 'Clients', 'Groups', 'Loans', and 'Savings'. Each populated group displays at least one result row showing the entity name, identifier and status. Results include items that match the entered partial substring and items whose casing differs from the typed term (demonstrating case-insensitive matching).

test_cases.test_case: Search that returns no matches displays the 'No results found' message
preconditions: User is logged in with access to the application (and permission to view Clients/Groups/Loans/Savings) and is on any in-app page.
No Client, Group, Loan or Savings entity contains <non-matching search term> in its name or identifier.
steps: 1. Click the search icon in the top toolbar to open the search input.
2. Enter <non-matching search term> in the search input.
test_cases.expected_result: "No results found" message is shown in the search results dropdown.

test_cases.test_case: Open a Client detail page from Global Search results
preconditions: User is logged in with access to the application (and permission to view Clients/Groups/Loans/Savings) and is on any in-app page.
A <Client> exists whose name or identifier matches <client search term>.
steps: 1. Click the search icon in the top toolbar to open the search input.
2. Enter <client search term> in the search input.
3. In the Clients group of the results dropdown, click the result row for the matching <Client>.
test_cases.expected_result: The <Client> detail page opens and the page header displays the client's name and identifier.

test_cases.test_case: Open a Group detail page from Global Search results
preconditions: User is logged in with access to the application (and permission to view Clients/Groups/Loans/Savings) and is on any in-app page.
A <Group> exists whose name or identifier matches <group search term>.
steps: 1. Click the search icon in the top toolbar to open the search input.
2. Enter <group search term> in the search input.
3. In the Groups group of the results dropdown, click the result row for the matching <Group>.
test_cases.expected_result: The <Group> detail page opens and the page header displays the group's name and identifier.

test_cases.test_case: Open a Loan detail page from Global Search results
preconditions: User is logged in with access to the application (and permission to view Clients/Groups/Loans/Savings) and is on any in-app page.
A <Loan> exists whose account number or related display name matches <loan search term>.
steps: 1. Click the search icon in the top toolbar to open the search input.
2. Enter <loan search term> in the search input.
3. In the Loans group of the results dropdown, click the result row for the matching <Loan>.
test_cases.expected_result: The <Loan> detail page opens and the page displays the loan account number and current status.

test_cases.test_case: Open a Savings detail page from Global Search results
preconditions: User is logged in with access to the application (and permission to view Clients/Groups/Loans/Savings) and is on any in-app page.
A <Savings> account exists whose account number or related display name matches <savings search term>.
steps: 1. Click the search icon in the top toolbar to open the search input.
2. Enter <savings search term> in the search input.
3. In the Savings group of the results dropdown, click the result row for the matching <Savings> account.
test_cases.expected_result: The <Savings> account detail page opens and the page displays the savings account number and current status.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user attempts to open Global Search
preconditions: User is not authenticated (not logged in)
steps: 1. Open the application root URL as an unauthenticated user
2. Attempt to click the top-bar search icon
test_cases.expected_result: Search input does not open; the user is redirected to the login page or shown the login screen and no search UI is focused or usable (the search action is blocked)

test_cases.test_case: Logged-in user without view permissions cannot open or use Global Search
preconditions: User is logged in with a role that lacks permission to view Clients, Groups, Loans, and Savings
steps: 1. Log in as a user whose role does not have view permission for Clients, Groups, Loans, or Savings
2. Navigate to any in-app page
3. Try to open Global Search by clicking the top-bar search icon
test_cases.expected_result: Search is not available: the top-bar search control is not visible or is disabled; clicking does not open the search input and no results dropdown appears (the search action is blocked due to missing permissions)

test_cases.test_case: Search does not return results from entity types the user lacks permission to view
preconditions: User is logged in and has view permission for Clients and Groups but does NOT have view permission for Loans and Savings
There exists an entity matching <search term> in the Loans scope
steps: 1. Log in as a user with permission to view Clients and Groups but without permission to view Loans or Savings
2. Click the top-bar search icon to open the search input
3. Enter <search term that matches an existing Loan entity> into the Search_Input
test_cases.expected_result: Loans and Savings groups are not shown in the results dropdown; Loan matches are not displayed. Results dropdown shows only groups and items from permitted scopes (Clients and Groups). If no permitted-scope matches exist, the dropdown shows the no-results state and the Loan matches remain hidden (search does not expose unpermitted entities)

test_cases.test_case: Attempting to open an entity detail from results is blocked when user lacks detail-view permission
preconditions: User is logged in and can invoke search but does NOT have permission to view Loan detail pages
There exists a Loan that matches <search term> and it appears in the search results list (visible as a result item)
steps: 1. Log in as a user who can run searches but does not have permission to open Loan detail pages
2. Click the top-bar search icon to open the search input
3. Enter <search term that matches the Loan> into the Search_Input
4. Click the matching Loan item in the results dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to the Loan detail page is blocked; the user remains on the current page and the Loan detail is not opened. An inline access-denied indicator or notification is shown (the click does not navigate to the detail page)

test_cases.test_case: Case-insensitive input returns matching entity when casing differs
preconditions: User is logged in and on any in-app page
An entity exists whose name differs from the test query only by letter case
steps: 1. Click the top-bar search icon
2. Type the entity name using a different letter case than the stored entity (e.g. mixed-case vs stored lowercase) into the search input
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: Results dropdown shows the matching entity under the correct group (Clients/Groups/Loans/Savings) with visible entity name and identifier (displayed name matches stored name without case differences shown as a visible match)

test_cases.test_case: Very short partial substring returns grouped matches
preconditions: User is logged in and on any in-app page
Multiple entities contain the single-character substring being tested
steps: 1. Click the top-bar search icon
2. Enter a single-character substring that appears in multiple entity names into the search input
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: Results dropdown updates and displays one or more matching items grouped by entity type (multiple groups or multiple items in a group may be visible); no UI error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Search with only special characters / emoji shows 'No results found' message and does not crash
preconditions: User is logged in and on any in-app page
No entity name contains the exact special-character/emoji string used
steps: 1. Click the top-bar search icon
2. Enter a string composed of special characters and/or emoji into the search input
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: Results dropdown displays the exact no-results message "No results found" and the UI remains responsive (no error dialogs or broken dropdown)

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in query is trimmed and returns matching entity
preconditions: User is logged in and on any in-app page
An entity exists whose name matches the trimmed version of the test query
steps: 1. Click the top-bar search icon
2. Type the entity name with leading and trailing spaces into the search input
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: Results dropdown shows the matching entity under the appropriate group; the displayed entity name and identifier do not include the leading/trailing spaces (visible result text is trimmed)

test_cases.test_case: Open Client Detail from Clients list
preconditions: User logged in as <User with Clients permission>
A client exists in the Clients list with Name = <existing client>
steps: 1. Navigate to Institution > Clients (or click Clients in the sidebar)
2. Enter <existing client> in the Search field
3. Click the client's Name link in the results
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page opens showing the client's name and a Status badge (e.g., 'Pending' (yellow) or 'Active' (green)) along with the account number and office

test_cases.test_case: Open Bulk Import page from Clients page
preconditions: User logged in as <User with Clients permission>
steps: 1. Navigate to Institution > Clients (or click Clients in the sidebar)
2. Click the 'Import Client' button at the top-right
test_cases.expected_result: Bulk Import page is shown with a 'Download Template' button and an Import History table visible

test_cases.test_case: Download client import template from Bulk Import page
preconditions: User logged in as <User with Clients permission>
Bulk Import page is accessible
steps: 1. Navigate to Institution > Clients and click 'Import Client' to open the Bulk Import page
2. Click the 'Download Template' button
test_cases.expected_result: Client Excel template download is triggered in the browser

test_cases.test_case: Open Create Client wizard from Clients page
preconditions: User logged in as <User with Clients permission>
steps: 1. Navigate to Institution > Clients
2. Click the 'Create Client' button
test_cases.expected_result: Create Client wizard opens and displays Step 1: 'General Details'

test_cases.test_case: Create client via wizard filling required General Details and submit (client becomes Pending)
preconditions: User logged in as <User with Clients permission>
steps: 1. Navigate to Institution > Clients and click 'Create Client' to open the wizard
2. In Step 1 'General Details', select <Office>, enter <First Name>, enter <Last Name>, and enter <Submitted On>
3. Click Next until the final step
4. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page opens showing the entered client name and a Status badge 'Pending' (yellow); Submitted On is displayed on the page

test_cases.test_case: Create client via wizard including Address Details (Step 2) and verify address appears on Client Detail
preconditions: User logged in as <User with Clients permission>
steps: 1. Navigate to Institution > Clients and click 'Create Client' to open the wizard
2. In Step 1 'General Details', fill required fields: select <Office>, enter <First Name>, enter <Last Name>, enter <Submitted On>
3. Click Next to go to Step 2 'Address Details'
4. Enter <address details> in the Address Details section
5. Click Next to proceed through remaining steps and click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page opens and the Address section displays the entered <address details>

test_cases.test_case: Create client via wizard including Family Members (Step 3) and verify family member appears on Client Detail
preconditions: User logged in as <User with Clients permission>
steps: 1. Navigate to Institution > Clients and click 'Create Client' to open the wizard
2. In Step 1 'General Details', fill required fields: select <Office>, enter <First Name>, enter <Last Name>, enter <Submitted On>
3. Click Next twice to reach Step 3 'Family Members'
4. Enter <family member details> in the Family Members section
5. Click Next to the final step and click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page opens and the Family Members tab contains the added <family member details>

test_cases.test_case: Create client via wizard including Identifiers (Step 4) and verify identifier listed in Identifiers tab
preconditions: User logged in as <User with Clients permission>
steps: 1. Navigate to Institution > Clients and click 'Create Client' to open the wizard
2. In Step 1 'General Details', fill required fields: select <Office>, enter <First Name>, enter <Last Name>, enter <Submitted On>
3. Click Next until Step 4 'Identifiers'
4. Enter <Document Type> and <Document Key> in the Identifiers section
5. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page opens and the Identifiers tab lists a row with the entered <Document Type> and <Document Key>

test_cases.test_case: Activate a Pending client by entering a valid Activation Date
preconditions: User logged in as <User with Clients permission>
A client exists in Pending state with Submitted_On = <submitted date>
steps: 1. Navigate to Institution > Clients and open the Pending client's Detail page
2. Click the 'Activate' action
3. Enter <activation date on or after submitted date> in Activation Date
4. Click Confirm (or Save) on the Activate dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page shows the Status badge 'Active' (green) and the Activation Date field displays <activation date on or after submitted date>

test_cases.test_case: Edit a Pending client and verify updated detail is shown
preconditions: User logged in as <User with Clients permission>
A client exists in Pending state
steps: 1. Navigate to Institution > Clients and open the Pending client's Detail page
2. Click the 'Edit' action
3. Update <Mobile Number> to <updated mobile number>
4. Click Save
test_cases.expected_result: Client Detail page displays the updated <Mobile Number> value

test_cases.test_case: Reject a Pending client by providing a reason
preconditions: User logged in as <User with Clients permission>
A client exists in Pending state
steps: 1. Navigate to Institution > Clients and open the Pending client's Detail page
2. Click the 'Reject' action

3. Enter a valid Short_Name into Step 1 (Details)
4. Click Next to move to Step 2 (Currency)
5. Click Next to move to Step 3 (Terms)
6. Click Next to move to Step 4 (Settings)
7. Click Next to move to Step 5 (Charges)
8. Use Charges_Search to locate a predefined charge (search action)
9. Add the found charge to Charges_List (repeating group) — first entry
10. Add a second charge to Charges_List — second entry
11. Remove the first charge from Charges_List
12. Remove the remaining charge so Charges_List is empty
13. Click Next to move to Step 6 (Accounting)
14. Click Save on the wizard
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: Save completes and navigates to the product detail page; product detail Charges section displays zero charges (Charges list shows no entries)

test_cases.test_case: After successful Save, press browser Back to check for pre-filled creation form / duplicate creation risk
preconditions: User is authenticated with product-management permissions
Savings Products page is open
steps: 1. Click "+ Create Savings Product"
2. Enter a valid Product_Name into Step 1 (Details)
3. Enter a valid Short_Name into Step 1 (Details)
4. Click Next until Step 6 (Accounting) is reached
5. Click Save to create the product
6. On the product detail page, press the browser Back button once
test_cases.expected_result: Succeeds: pressing Back returns the user to the Savings Products listing or a blank Create form (the Create form is not pre-filled with the just-submitted values); the listing shows the created product but there is no visual duplication of the product record on the listing page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to jump directly to Settings step without filling required Details fields
preconditions: User is authenticated with product-management permissions
Savings Products page is open
steps: 1. Click "+ Create Savings Product"
2. Attempt to click the stepper tab for Step 4 (Settings) directly without entering any values in Step 1
3. Observe the active step after the click
test_cases.expected_result: Is blocked / error shown: navigation to Step 4 is blocked; the wizard remains on Step 1 (Details) and Product_Name and Short_Name fields show required-field validation indicators/errors (visible inline on Step 1)

test_cases.test_case: Create Share Product (Accounting Method = None) via 7-step wizard
preconditions: Authenticated user with product-management privileges and access to the Products section; the application context is on the Share Products listing page.
steps: 1. Click '+ Create Share Product' on the Share Products listing page to open the Create Share Product wizard
2. On Details (Step 1): Enter <Product Name> in Product Name, <Short Name> in Short Name, and <Description> in Description; click Next
3. On Currency (Step 2): select <Currency> and fill any optional currency fields as needed; click Next
4. On Terms (Step 3): enter <Total_Number_Of_Shares> in Total Number of Shares and <Nominal_Unit_Price> in Nominal/Unit Price (fill Shares To Be Issued if desired); click Next
5. On Settings (Step 4): set any desired settings (optionally check Allow Dividends for Inactive Clients); click Next
6. On Market Price (Step 5): optionally click Add Row and add a market price row or leave blank; click Next
7. On Charges (Step 6): optionally add <Charge> via the search-and-add; click Next
8. On Accounting (Step 7): select 'None' for Accounting Method; click Finish/Create
test_cases.expected_result: A new row appears in the Share Products table with Product Name <Product Name>, Short Name <Short Name>, and Total Shares <Total_Number_Of_Shares>

test_cases.test_case: Create Share Product with Accounting Method = Cash-based and enter GL mappings
preconditions: Authenticated user with product-management privileges and access to the Products section; the application context is on the Share Products listing page.
steps: 1. Click '+ Create Share Product' to open the Create Share Product wizard
2. On Details (Step 1): enter required fields: <Product Name>, <Short Name>, <Description>; click Next
3. On Currency (Step 2): fill required or optional currency fields as needed; click Next
4. On Terms (Step 3): enter required fields: <Total_Number_Of_Shares> and <Nominal_Unit_Price>; click Next
5. On Settings (Step 4): configure any needed settings; click Next
6. On Market Price (Step 5): optionally add rows or skip; click Next
7. On Charges (Step 6): optionally add charges; click Next

8. On Accounting (Step 7): select 'Cash-based' for Accounting Method; when GL mapping fields appear, enter <Share_Reference>, <Share_Suspense>, <Equity_in_Shares>, <Income_from_Fees>, and <Share_Equity>; click Finish/Create
9. In the Share Products listing, open the newly created product's detail view by clicking its Product Name link
test_cases.expected_result: A new row appears in the Share Products table with Product Name <Product Name> and Short Name <Short Name>; when opening its Detail view, the Accounting section shows 'Cash-based' selected and displays the entered GL mappings for Share Reference, Share Suspense, Equity in Shares, Income from Fees, and Share Equity

steps: 1. Open the detail view for an existing charge by clicking its Name in the Charges table

test_cases.expected_result: The Delete action/button is not visible or accessible in the charge detail action bar; the user cannot delete the charge and the charge remains unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: Create charge when Charge_Applies_To has no Charge_Time_Type options (Client)
preconditions: User is logged in with an active organization selected and has permissions to manage product/charge definitions (admin/configuration role)
steps: 1. Click the "+ Create Charge" button
2. Select "Client" in the Charge_Applies_To dropdown
3. Enter a valid value into the Currency field
4. Enter a valid numeric value into the Amount field
5. Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; charge detail view opens and displays the created charge where the Charge Time Type value is visibly empty/none (no selected option shown)

test_cases.test_case: Very long Charge Name (>200 characters) is rejected at submission
preconditions: User is logged in with an active organization selected and has permissions to manage product/charge definitions (admin/configuration role)
steps: 1. Click the "+ Create Charge" button
2. Enter a very long string (>200 characters) into the Charge_Name field
3. Select "Loan" in the Charge_Applies_To dropdown
4. Enter a valid value into the Currency field
5. Select a valid Charge_Calculation_Type
6. Enter a valid numeric value into the Amount field
7. Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Charge_Name field displays an inline error indicating the value exceeds allowed length and the form is blocked from submitting

test_cases.test_case: Amount entered with excessive decimal precision is persisted with visible rounding
preconditions: User is logged in with an active organization selected and has permissions to manage product/charge definitions (admin/configuration role)
steps: 1. Click the "+ Create Charge" button
2. Enter a valid value into the Charge_Name field
3. Select "Savings Account" in the Charge_Applies_To dropdown
4. Enter a valid value into the Currency field
5. Select a valid Charge_Calculation_Type
6. Enter an Amount value containing a high number of decimal places
7. Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; charge detail view opens and displays the Amount rounded to two decimal places (visible rounded value in the detail view)

test_cases.test_case: Open Create Floating Rate form from listing
preconditions: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings and the application is at the Products/Floating Rates listing view
steps: 1. Click the '+ Create Floating Rate' button in the Floating Rates listing toolbar
test_cases.expected_result: The Floating Rate Creation form opens and displays the required fields: Floating Rate Name (marked required), Is Base Lending Rate checkbox, Is Active checkbox, and a Rate Periods section with controls to add rate period rows (From Date, Interest Rate, Is Differential Rate)

test_cases.test_case: Create a new floating rate with one rate period (non-base)
preconditions: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings and the application is at the Products/Floating Rates listing view
No pre-existing conflicting base lending rate is assumed (or the test chooses not to mark the new rate as base)
steps: 1. Click the '+ Create Floating Rate' button to open the creation form
2. Enter <valid floating rate name> in the Floating Rate Name field
3. Leave 'Is Base Lending Rate' unchecked
4. Toggle 'Is Active' to <active state> (check or uncheck as desired)
5. Click 'Add Row' in the Rate Periods section
6. Enter <From Date> in the new rate period's From Date field
7. Enter <Interest Rate> in the new rate period's Interest Rate field
8. Leave 'Is Differential Rate' unchecked for the new period
9. Click the 'Create Floating Rate' submit button
test_cases.expected_result: The Floating Rates listing is shown and contains a new row for <valid floating rate name>; the row displays the chosen Is Active state and the Created By column shows the current user, and the Rate Periods for the new floating rate are available from its detail view

test_cases.test_case: View Floating Rate detail from listing
preconditions: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings and the application is at the Products/Floating Rates listing view
At least one floating rate exists in the listing as <existing floating rate>
steps: 1. In the Floating Rates table, click the 'View' action (or click the Floating Rate Name link) for <existing floating rate>
test_cases.expected_result: The Floating Rate detail page for <existing floating rate> is displayed showing the floating rate name as the page heading and a Rate Periods history table listing rows with From Date, Interest Rate and whether each row is marked as a differential rate

test_cases.test_case: Open Edit Floating Rate form from listing
preconditions: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings and the application is at the Products/Floating Rates listing view
At least one floating rate exists in the listing as <existing floating rate>
steps: 1. In the Floating Rates table, click the 'Edit' row action for <existing floating rate>
test_cases.expected_result: The Floating Rate Edit form opens for <existing floating rate> and is pre-populated showing the Floating Rate Name, Is Base Lending Rate checkbox, Is Active checkbox, and the existing Rate Periods rows with From Date and Interest Rate values

test_cases.test_case: Open Edit Floating Rate form from detail view
preconditions: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings and the application is on the Floating Rate detail page for <existing floating rate>
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit' action on the Floating Rate detail page
test_cases.expected_result: The Floating Rate Edit form opens for <existing floating rate> and is pre-populated with the current Floating Rate Name, Is Base Lending Rate state, Is Active state, and the list of Rate Periods visible for editing

test_cases.test_case: Modify a floating rate (add a rate period) and save changes
preconditions: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings and the application is at the Products/Floating Rates listing view
At least one floating rate exists in the listing as <existing floating rate>
steps: 1. In the Floating Rates table, click the 'Edit' row action for <existing floating rate> to open the edit form
2. Optionally modify the Floating Rate Name to <updated floating rate name> or toggle the 'Is Active' checkbox to a different state
3. Click 'Add Row' in the Rate Periods section
4. Enter <From Date> in the newly added rate period's From Date field
5. Enter <Interest Rate> in the newly added rate period's Interest Rate field
6. Set or leave 'Is Differential Rate' as appropriate for the new period
7. Click the 'Save Changes' button
test_cases.expected_result: After saving, the UI returns to the Floating Rate detail/listing and the floating rate shows the updated values: the Floating Rate Name displays as <updated floating rate name> (if changed) and the Rate Periods list now includes the newly added period with the entered From Date and Interest Rate

test_cases.test_case: Create Floating Rate - required Floating Rate Name left blank

User is at the Products > Floating Rates listing view
steps: 1. Click the '+ Create Floating Rate' button
2. Leave the Floating Rate Name field blank
3. (Optional) Toggle other controls or add a Rate Period row if available
4. Click the 'Create Floating Rate' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Floating Rate Name field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; no floating rate is created; user remains on the creation form.

test_cases.test_case: Edit Floating Rate - required Floating Rate Name left blank
preconditions: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings
An existing Floating Rate is available to edit
User is at the Floating Rate detail or listing where Edit is available
steps: 1. Open an existing Floating Rate detail view or select its row in the listing
2. Click the 'Edit' action to open the edit form
3. Clear the Floating Rate Name field (leave it blank)
4. Click the 'Save Changes' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Floating Rate Name field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; the floating rate is not updated; user remains on the edit form.

test_cases.test_case: Create Floating Rate - invalid From Date in Rate Periods
preconditions: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings
User is at the Products > Floating Rates listing view

3. Add a Rate Period row if none exists
4. Enter <invalid date> in the From Date field of the Rate Period row
5. Click the 'Create Floating Rate' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the From Date field indicating the date is invalid; the form does not submit; no floating rate is created; user remains on the creation form and the invalid From Date field is highlighted.

test_cases.test_case: Create Floating Rate - non-numeric Interest Rate in Rate Period
preconditions: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings
User is at the Products > Floating Rates listing view
steps: 1. Click the '+ Create Floating Rate' button
2. Fill the Floating Rate Name with <valid name>
3. Add a Rate Period row if none exists
4. Enter <non-numeric interest value> in the Interest Rate field of the Rate Period row
5. Click the 'Create Floating Rate' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Interest Rate field indicating a numeric value is required; the form does not submit; no floating rate is created; user remains on the creation form and the invalid Interest Rate field is highlighted.

test_cases.test_case: Create Floating Rate - attempt to set Is Base Lending Rate while another base rate exists
preconditions: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings
An existing Floating Rate already exists and is marked as the base lending rate
User is at the Products > Floating Rates listing view
steps: 1. Click the '+ Create Floating Rate' button
2. Enter <valid name> in the Floating Rate Name field
3. Check the Is Base Lending Rate checkbox
4. Click the 'Create Floating Rate' button
test_cases.expected_result: The create action is blocked; an inline or form-level validation indicator appears on the Is Base Lending Rate control or on the form explaining that only one base lending rate can exist at a time; the form does not submit; no new base floating rate is created; user remains on the creation form.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot open Create Floating Rate form
preconditions: User is not logged in
steps: 1. Navigate to the Products > Floating Rates listing URL without authenticating
2. Attempt to click the '+ Create Floating Rate' button (or navigate to the creation form URL)
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page or presented with the authentication prompt; the Floating Rate creation form is not displayed; no access to create functionality is granted.

test_cases.test_case: User without permissions cannot see or open '+ Create Floating Rate' action
preconditions: User is logged in WITHOUT permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings
User is at the Products > Floating Rates listing view
steps: 1. Observe the top action area of the Floating Rates listing
test_cases.expected_result: The '+ Create Floating Rate' button is not visible (or is disabled) to the user without permissions; the user cannot open the creation form.

test_cases.test_case: Create a base lending rate when no base exists (at-limit pass)
preconditions: No existing floating rate has Is_Base_Lending_Rate checked (system state: zero base rates)
steps: 1. Click + Create Floating Rate
2. Enter a valid value in Floating Rate Name
3. Check Is_Base_Lending_Rate
4. Click Create Floating Rate
test_cases.expected_result: Creation succeeds and returns to the listing; the new row appears in the table with Is Base Lending Rate shown as checked

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to create a second base lending rate when one already exists (just-past-limit fail)
preconditions: An existing floating rate record is present in the listing with Is_Base_Lending_Rate checked (system state: one base already exists)
steps: 1. Click + Create Floating Rate
2. Enter a valid value in Floating Rate Name
3. Check Is_Base_Lending_Rate
4. Click Create Floating Rate
test_cases.expected_result: Creation is blocked; a visible inline error or top-level form error is shown indicating the Is_Base_Lending_Rate cannot be set because a base lending rate already exists, and the creation form remains open

test_cases.test_case: Edit another floating rate to set Is_Base_Lending_Rate when a different base exists (just-past-limit fail)
preconditions: An existing floating rate record A has Is_Base_Lending_Rate checked; another floating rate record B exists without Is_Base_Lending_Rate checked
steps: 1. From the listing, click Edit on floating rate record B
2. Check Is_Base_Lending_Rate in the edit form
3. Click Save Changes
test_cases.expected_result: Save is blocked; a visible inline error is shown on the Is_Base_Lending_Rate control or a top-level form error is shown explaining another base rate exists, and the edit form remains open with no change saved

test_cases.test_case: Enter an extremely long Floating Rate Name (200+ characters) on creation
preconditions: User is at the Products/Floating Rates listing view
steps: 1. Click + Create Floating Rate
2. Enter a string longer than 200 characters into Floating Rate Name
3. Click Create Floating Rate
test_cases.expected_result: Creation is blocked; an inline validation error is shown on Floating Rate Name indicating the value exceeds the allowed maximum length

test_cases.test_case: Use emoji and extended Unicode characters in Floating Rate Name
preconditions: User is at the Products/Floating Rates listing view
steps: 1. Click + Create Floating Rate
2. Enter a Floating Rate Name containing emoji and extended Unicode characters
3. Click Create Floating Rate
test_cases.expected_result: Creation succeeds; listing and detail view display the same Unicode characters (the name is accepted and visible as entered)

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing whitespace in Floating Rate Name is trimmed on save
preconditions: User is at the Products/Floating Rates listing view
steps: 1. Click + Create Floating Rate
2. Enter a Floating Rate Name with leading and trailing spaces
3. Click Create Floating Rate
test_cases.expected_result: Creation succeeds; the listing/detail shows the Floating Rate Name with leading and trailing whitespace removed (trimmed) in the visible UI

test_cases.test_case: Enter Interest Rate with excessive decimal precision in a Rate Period row
preconditions: User is at the Products/Floating Rates listing view; creation form accepts Rate Period rows with an Interest_Rate numeric field
steps: 1. Click + Create Floating Rate
2. Enter a valid value in Floating Rate Name
3. Add a Rate Period row
4. In the Interest Rate field of the new row, enter a numeric value with a high number of decimal places
5. Click Create Floating Rate
test_cases.expected_result: Creation is blocked; an inline validation error is shown on the Interest Rate field indicating the entered decimal precision/value is invalid, and the form remains open

test_cases.test_case: Attempt duplicate submission by using browser Back after successful create (rapid duplicate-submit)
preconditions: No existing floating rate with the same Floating Rate Name exists prior to the test
steps: 1. Click + Create Floating Rate
2. Enter a valid value in Floating Rate Name
3. Click Create Floating Rate
4. When the listing is shown (creation success), use the browser Back button to return to the creation form
5. Click Create Floating Rate again without changing the form contents
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission is blocked or deduplicated; only one row with that Floating Rate Name appears in the listing and no duplicate record is created (visible listing shows a single entry)

test_cases.test_case: Open a delinquency range (Classification) from the Ranges table

At least one loan product exists to which buckets can be linked
Delinquency Ranges page is accessible from the Delinquency Management area

2. Locate the row in the Delinquency Ranges table with Classification <Classification>
3. Click the Classification link for that row
test_cases.expected_result: The Delinquency Range details (classification view) opens showing a heading with <Classification> and displaying the configured Minimum Age Days as <minimum age days> and Maximum Age Days as <maximum age days> (or a blank/empty Maximum Age Days cell if none was set)

test_cases.test_case: Create a new Delinquency Range with Minimum and Maximum days

At least one loan product exists to which buckets can be linked
Delinquency Ranges page is accessible from the Delinquency Management area
steps: 1. Navigate to Delinquency Management -> Delinquency Ranges
2. Click the control to open the Create Delinquency Range form
3. Enter <Classification> in the Classification field
4. Enter <minimum age days> in the Minimum Age Days field
5. Enter <maximum age days> in the Maximum Age Days field
6. Click Create (submit) on the form
test_cases.expected_result: A new row appears in the Delinquency Ranges table showing Classification <Classification> with Minimum Age Days <minimum age days> and Maximum Age Days <maximum age days>

test_cases.test_case: Open a delinquency bucket from the Buckets table

At least one loan product exists to which buckets can be linked
Delinquency Buckets page is accessible from the Delinquency Management area
steps: 1. Navigate to Delinquency Management -> Delinquency Buckets
2. Locate the row in the Delinquency Buckets table with Bucket Name <Bucket Name>
3. Click the Bucket Name link for that row
test_cases.expected_result: The Delinquency Bucket details page opens showing a heading with <Bucket Name> and listing the bucket's associated ranges, each with its Range Label and Minimum/Maximum Age Days as visible rows

test_cases.test_case: Create a Delinquency Bucket with multiple Bucket Ranges using the Add Row interface
preconditions: User logged in as <Admin> (authenticated user with configuration/admin privileges)
At least one loan product exists to which buckets can be linked
Delinquency Buckets page is accessible from the Delinquency Management area
steps: 1. Navigate to Delinquency Management -> Delinquency Buckets
2. Click the control to open the Create Delinquency Bucket form
3. Enter <Bucket Name> in the Bucket Name field
4. Click Add Row to add a new Bucket Range

5. In the first new range row, enter <Range Label 1> in Range Label, enter <minimum age days 1> in Minimum Age Days, enter <maximum age days 1> in Maximum Age Days (or leave blank to apply beyond minimum)
6. Click Add Row to add a second Bucket Range
7. In the second range row, enter <Range Label 2> in Range Label, enter <minimum age days 2> in Minimum Age Days, enter <maximum age days 2> in Maximum Age Days (or leave blank)
8. (Optional) Repeat Add Row and fill additional ranges as needed
9. Click Create (submit) on the form
test_cases.expected_result: A new row appears in the Delinquency Buckets table with Bucket Name <Bucket Name> and the bucket entry displays the configured ranges: each listed range shows its Range Label and the corresponding Minimum Age Days and Maximum Age Days (Maximum Age Days cell shown blank for any range where Maximum was left empty)

test_cases.test_case: Create Delinquency Range — required fields left blank

At least one loan product exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Delinquency Ranges page
2. Click the control to open the Create Delinquency Range form
3. Leave the Classification field blank
4. Leave the Minimum Age Days field blank
5. Click Create
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; Classification field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; Minimum Age Days field displays an inline validation error indicating it is required; no delinquency range is created or added to the table

test_cases.test_case: Submit both Create forms with all required fields empty

At least one loan product exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Delinquency Ranges page
2. Open the Create Delinquency Range form
3. Leave all required fields in the form empty (Classification and Minimum Age Days)
4. Click Create
5. Navigate to the Delinquency Buckets page
6. Open the Create Delinquency Bucket form
7. Leave Bucket Name blank
8. Click Add Range to add one Bucket Range row
9. In the new Bucket Range row leave Range Label blank and leave Minimum Age Days blank
10. Click Create
test_cases.expected_result: On the Delinquency Range form submit: form does not submit and the Classification and Minimum Age Days fields show inline required-field validation errors; on the Delinquency Bucket form submit: form does not submit and the Bucket Name field shows an inline required-field validation error and the added Bucket Range row shows inline validation errors on Range Label and Minimum Age Days; no ranges or buckets are created

test_cases.test_case: Create Delinquency Range — non-numeric input in Minimum Age Days
preconditions: User is authenticated with configuration/admin privileges
At least one loan product exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Delinquency Ranges page
2. Open the Create Delinquency Range form
3. Enter a valid value in Classification
4. Enter <non-numeric input> into the Minimum Age Days field
5. Click Create
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; Minimum Age Days field displays an inline validation error indicating the value must be numeric; no delinquency range is created

test_cases.test_case: Create Delinquency Bucket — non-numeric input in Bucket Range Maximum Age Days
preconditions: User is authenticated with configuration/admin privileges
At least one loan product exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Delinquency Buckets page
2. Open the Create Delinquency Bucket form
3. Enter a valid Bucket Name
4. Click Add Range to add one Bucket Range row
5. Enter a valid Range Label
6. Enter a valid numeric value in Minimum Age Days
7. Enter <non-numeric input> into Maximum Age Days for that range
8. Click Create
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; Maximum Age Days field in the Bucket Range row displays an inline validation error indicating the value must be numeric; no bucket is created

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated access to Delinquency pages is blocked

steps: 1. Attempt to navigate directly to the Delinquency Ranges page URL
2. Attempt to navigate directly to the Delinquency Buckets page URL
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked; user is redirected to the application's login page (or shown the login screen) when trying to open either Delinquency Ranges or Delinquency Buckets; no create forms or tables are accessible without authentication

test_cases.test_case: Create action blocked when user lacks configuration/admin privileges
preconditions: User is authenticated but does NOT have configuration/admin privileges

2. Navigate to the Delinquency Buckets page
3. Open the Create Delinquency Bucket form
4. Fill all required fields with valid values
5. Click Create
test_cases.expected_result: Creation is blocked; submission does not succeed and a visible authorization/global error is shown indicating the user lacks sufficient privileges to create delinquency buckets; no bucket is created

test_cases.test_case: Create action blocked when no Loan Products exist to link
preconditions: User is authenticated with configuration/admin privileges
No Loan Products exist in the system

2. Open the Create Delinquency Bucket form
3. Fill Bucket Name and add at least one Bucket Range with valid Range Label and Minimum Age Days
4. Click Create
test_cases.expected_result: Form submission is blocked; a visible global or inline error indicates that creation is not allowed because there are no Loan Products available to link the delinquency bucket to; no bucket is created

test_cases.test_case: Create Delinquency Range with Maximum Age Days left blank (open-ended range)

Delinquency Ranges page is open
steps: 1. Click Create Delinquency Range

3. Enter a numeric value in Minimum_Age_Days
4. Leave Maximum_Age_Days blank
5. Click Create

test_cases.test_case: Create Delinquency Range where Maximum Age Days equals Minimum Age Days (single-day range)

test_cases.test_case: Create Delinquency Bucket with one bucket range having Maximum Age Days blank (repeating group open-ended item)
preconditions: User is authenticated with configuration/admin privileges
Create Delinquency Bucket form is open
steps: 1. Enter a value in Bucket_Name
2. In Bucket_Ranges repeating group, add first item: enter a value in Range_Label
3. Enter a numeric value in the first item's Minimum_Age_Days

test_cases.test_case: Add then remove all Bucket_Ranges entries and submit the Create Bucket form

Create Delinquency Bucket form is open

test_cases.test_case: Very long text in Classification and Bucket Name (200+ characters)

test_cases.test_case: Special characters and emoji in Classification and Range_Label

test_cases.test_case: Enter Minimum_Age_Days greater than Maximum_Age_Days in Create Delinquency Range

test_cases.test_case: Create new loan application via multi-step wizard and submit
preconditions: User logged in as <role> with loan-management permissions
Viewing <Client Detail> page for <target client>
steps: 1. Click 'New Loan' on the <Client Detail> page to open the Loan Application wizard

2. On Step 1 (Details): Select <Product_Name>; enter <Principal> within product bounds, <Number_Of_Repayments>, <Repaid_Every_Number>, select <Repaid_Every_Unit>, enter <Interest_Rate> within product bounds, select <Loan_Officer>, enter <Submitted_On> and <Expected_Disbursement_Date>, enter <External_ID>
3. Click Next to go to Step 2 (Terms)
4. On Step 2 (Terms): accept or adjust the prefilled <Repayment_Strategy>, <Amortization>, <Interest_Method>, <Interest_Calculation_Period> and enter <Grace_Period_Days> as needed
5. Click Next to go to Step 3 (Charges)
6. On Step 3 (Charges): review inherited charges (no change)
7. Click Next to go to Step 4 (Collateral)
8. On Step 4 (Collateral): leave optional collateral empty
9. Click Submit on the wizard
test_cases.expected_result: Loan Detail page opens; status badge displays 'Submitted and Pending Approval' (yellow)

test_cases.test_case: Enter and persist Terms (Step 2) in Loan Application wizard
preconditions: User logged in as <role> with loan-management permissions
Viewing <Client Detail> page for <target client>
steps: 1. Click 'New Loan' to open the Loan Application wizard
2. On Step 1 (Details): select <Product_Name> and fill required fields minimally to proceed (enter <Principal>, <Number_Of_Repayments>, <Interest_Rate>, <Submitted_On>)
3. Click Next to go to Step 2 (Terms)
4. On Step 2 (Terms): select <Repayment_Strategy>, select <Amortization>, select <Interest_Method>, select <Interest_Calculation_Period>, enter <Grace_Period_Days>
5. Click Next through Steps 3 and 4 (use defaults or no changes)
6. Click Submit on the wizard
test_cases.expected_result: On the Loan Detail page, the Terms section displays the selected <Repayment_Strategy> and <Amortization> entered during the wizard

test_cases.test_case: Add a charge in Step 3 (Charges) of the Loan Application wizard and persist it
preconditions: User logged in as <role> with loan-management permissions
Viewing <Client Detail> page for <target client>
steps: 1. Click 'New Loan' to open the Loan Application wizard
2. On Step 1 (Details): select <Product_Name> and fill required fields minimally

steps: 1. Open the Share Account Detail page for an account in Active state
2. Look for the Approve action in the action bar
test_cases.expected_result: Approve action button is not visible in the action bar while the account is in Active state; user cannot open an Approve dialog from Active

test_cases.test_case: Requested_Shares = product minimum (exact boundary) is accepted on submit
preconditions: User is logged in with permission to manage client accounts
User is viewing a specific Client Detail page
At least one Share Product exists with a defined minimum and maximum shares per client
steps: 1. Click Create Share Account / Open Share Account Application form on the Client Detail page
2. Select a Share Product that has a known minimum allowed shares
3. Enter <minimum allowed value> in the Requested_Shares field
4. Fill all other required fields on the form (e.g., Submitted_On, Application_Date or Savings_Account_For_Charges if required)
5. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Submit succeeds; the newly created account appears on the client detail with status badge showing 'Submitted and Pending Approval' and the detail view shows Total Pending Shares equal to <minimum allowed value>

test_cases.test_case: Requested_Shares one unit below product minimum is blocked with inline validation
preconditions: User is logged in with permission to manage client accounts
User is viewing a specific Client Detail page
At least one Share Product exists with a defined minimum allowed shares
steps: 1. Click Create Share Account / Open Share Account Application form on the Client Detail page
2. Select the Share Product used to determine the product minimum
3. Enter <one unit below minimum allowed value> in the Requested_Shares field
4. Fill all other required fields on the form
5. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked; the Requested_Shares field displays an inline validation error and the form remains on the application page (no account is created)

test_cases.test_case: Requested_Shares = product maximum (exact boundary) is accepted on submit

steps: 1. Click Create Share Account / Open Share Account Application form on the Client Detail page

test_cases.test_case: Requested_Shares one unit above product maximum is blocked with inline validation

steps: 1. Click Create Share Account / Open Share Account Application form on the Client Detail page

test_cases.test_case: External_ID with leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed in saved view

test_cases.test_case: Add charges then remove all entries in Charges repeating group before submit
preconditions: User is logged in with permission to manage client accounts
User is viewing a specific Client Detail page
Create Share Account form is open and Charges repeating group is present
steps: 1. In the Create Share Account form, add one Charges repeating group entry
2. Remove the previously added Charges entry so the repeating group has zero items
3. Fill all other required fields on the form (including a valid Requested_Shares within product bounds)
4. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: Submit succeeds; the created account appears with status badge 'Submitted and Pending Approval' and the account Charges tab/table displays zero rows (no charges listed)

test_cases.test_case: Approve then immediately Activate: rapid consecutive state transitions from Pending to Active
preconditions: User is logged in with permission to manage client accounts
A share account application exists for the client and is in 'Pending' status
steps: 1. On the share account detail page with status 'Pending', click the Approve action
2. Enter a valid value in Approved_Shares (required field)
3. Enter a valid Approved_Date (required field)
4. Submit the Approve action
5. Immediately after Approve completes and the UI shows the updated status, click the Activate action
test_cases.expected_result: Both transitions succeed; after Approve the status badge changes to 'Approved', and immediately invoking Activate transitions the account to 'Active' where the status badge shows 'Active'

test_cases.test_case: Create Fixed Deposit - happy path
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
An active client is selected on the Client Detail page and relevant FD/RD products and interest rate charts are configured in the system.
steps: 1. On the Client Detail page, click the 'Create Fixed Deposit' button to open the Fixed Deposit creation form
2. Select <Fixed Deposit Product> from the Fixed Deposit Product dropdown
3. Enter <Deposit Amount> in the Deposit Amount field
4. Enter <Deposit_Period_Value> and select <Deposit_Period_Unit> (Days/Months/Years)
5. Select <Maturity Instruction> from the Maturity Instructions dropdown
6. Verify the Interest Rate is displayed (computed from product/amount/term)
7. Click the 'Create Fixed Deposit' submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Fixed Deposit account created; detail page shows deposit amount, maturity date, maturity amount, interest rate, and status.

test_cases.test_case: Create Recurring Deposit - happy path
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
An active client is selected on the Client Detail page and relevant FD/RD products and interest rate charts are configured in the system.
steps: 1. On the Client Detail page, click the 'Create Recurring Deposit' button to open the Recurring Deposit creation form
2. Select <Recurring Deposit Product> from the Recurring Deposit Product dropdown
3. Enter <Mandatory_Deposit_Amount> in the Mandatory Deposit Amount field
4. Enter <Deposit_Period_Value> and select <Deposit_Period_Unit> (Days/Months/Years)
5. Select <Deposit_Frequency> from the Deposit Frequency dropdown
6. Enter <Expected_First_Deposit_On> in the Expected First Deposit On date field
7. Verify the Interest Rate is displayed (computed from product/amount/term/frequency)
8. Click the 'Create Recurring Deposit' submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Recurring Deposit account created; detail page shows deposit schedule, total deposits made, maturity details, and interest rate.

test_cases.test_case: View GL account detail from Chart of Accounts
preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has Accounting access and an organization instance configured; Accounting module accessible.
<existing GL account> exists in the Chart of Accounts
steps: 1. Navigate to Accounting → Chart of Accounts
2. In the accounts tree, click the Account Name of <existing GL account>
3. Wait for the GL account detail view to load
test_cases.expected_result: The GL account detail view opens showing the account heading with the Account Name and GL Code; the Account Type badge is visible (color-coded), Manual Entries Allowed indicator, Usage (Header or Detail), and Description are displayed; Edit and Delete actions are visible on the detail page.

test_cases.test_case: Create a new Detail GL account via Create GL Account form
preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has Accounting access and an organization instance configured; Accounting module accessible.
steps: 1. Navigate to Accounting → Chart of Accounts
2. Click '+ Create GL Account' to open the Create GL Account form
3. Select <Account Type> from the Account Type dropdown
4. Enter <unique GL code> in the GL Code field
5. Enter <Account Name> in the Account Name field
6. Select <Detail> for Account Usage
7. Check the Manual Entries Allowed checkbox
8. (Optional) Select <Tag> from the Tag dropdown
9. Click Create
test_cases.expected_result: A new row appears in the Chart of Accounts hierarchy under the selected <Account Type> showing the entered GL Code and Account Name; the Account Type badge on the row is color-coded according to the selected type (Assets → blue, Liabilities → red, Equity → green, Income → purple, Expenses → orange); the Manual Entries Allowed indicator is shown on the new row.

test_cases.test_case: Create GL account — Parent Account dropdown lists only header accounts of selected Account Type
preconditions: User is logged in with a role that has Accounting access and an organization instance configured; Accounting module accessible.
There are one or more header accounts in the Chart of Accounts for the selected <Account Type>
steps: 1. Navigate to Accounting → Chart of Accounts
2. Click '+ Create GL Account' to open the Create GL Account form
3. Select <Account Type> from the Account Type dropdown
4. Open the Parent Account dropdown to view available options
5. Select an appropriate header account from the list
6. Enter <unique GL code> in the GL Code field and <Account Name> in the Account Name field
7. Click Create
test_cases.expected_result: The Parent Account dropdown displays only header accounts whose Account Type equals the selected <Account Type>; after creation, the new account appears as a child under the selected parent in the Chart of Accounts tree.

test_cases.test_case: Edit an existing GL account and save changes

<existing GL account> exists in the Chart of Accounts

2. Click the Account Name of <existing GL account> to open its detail view
3. Click Edit to open the Edit GL Account form
4. Change Account Name to <updated Account Name>
5. Toggle the Manual Entries Allowed checkbox to the desired state
6. (Optional) Update Tag to <Tag>
7. Click Save
test_cases.expected_result: The GL account detail view displays the updated Account Name and the Manual Entries Allowed indicator reflects the change; the Chart of Accounts row for the account shows the updated Account Name.

test_cases.test_case: Create: GL Code uniqueness constraint violated (duplicate GL Code)

steps: 1. Navigate to Accounting → Chart of Accounts

4. Enter <existing GL Code> in GL Code (a GL Code already present in the system)
5. Enter <valid Account Name> in Account Name
6. Click Create
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; GL Code field displays a validation error indicating the GL Code is a duplicate and must be unique; GL account is not created

test_cases.test_case: Edit: GL Code (required) cleared and Save attempted

steps: 1. From the Chart of Accounts, open an existing GL account's detail view

test_cases.test_case: User without Accounting role cannot see Edit/Delete actions on GL account detail

test_cases.expected_result: Edit and Delete actions are not available in the GL account detail view for this user (buttons are not visible or disabled); attempting to invoke those actions is blocked and no edit/delete forms are opened

test_cases.test_case: Create: very long Account_Name (200+ characters) handling

test_cases.test_case: Open Add Journal Entry form
