steps: 1. Navigate to Accounting → Journal Entries
2. Click the '+ Add Journal Entry' button in the Journal Entries table
test_cases.expected_result: The Journal Entry form opens and is visible; the form displays fields Office, Currency, Transaction Date, Entry Lines (with an 'Add Row' button), Comments, and a 'Create Journal Entry' submit button.

test_cases.test_case: Create Journal Entry with balanced debits and credits
preconditions: Logged in with Accounting privileges, at least one Office and relevant GL accounts configured, and access to the Accounting section of the application.
steps: 1. Navigate to Accounting → Journal Entries
2. Click '+ Add Journal Entry' to open the Journal Entry form
3. Select <Office> in the Office field
4. Select <Currency> in the Currency field
5. Enter <Transaction_Date> in the Transaction Date field
6. (optional) Enter <Reference_Number> in the Reference Number field
7. In Entry Lines: select <valid GL account A> for the first row, set Type to Debit, enter <amount X> in Amount
8. Click 'Add Row', select <valid GL account B> for the second row, set Type to Credit, enter <amount X> in Amount
9. (optional) Enter <Comments> in Comments
10. Click 'Create Journal Entry'
test_cases.expected_result: A new row appears in the Journal Entries table showing the entered Transaction Date, Office, and Reference Number (if provided); the created entry's Debit and Credit totals are visible and show balanced amounts for the entry.

test_cases.test_case: Entry lines totals and difference update when adding rows in the Journal Entry form
preconditions: Logged in with Accounting privileges, at least one Office and relevant GL accounts configured, and access to the Accounting section of the application.
steps: 1. Navigate to Accounting → Journal Entries
2. Click '+ Add Journal Entry' to open the Journal Entry form
3. Select <Office> and <Currency>, and enter <Transaction_Date>
4. In Entry Lines: select <valid GL account A>, set Type to Debit, enter <amount A> in Amount
5. Click 'Add Row', select <valid GL account B>, set Type to Credit, enter <amount A> in Amount
6. Observe the running totals area within the form
test_cases.expected_result: The Debit total and Credit total in the form update to reflect the entered entry lines; the Difference displays 0 (zero) indicating totals are balanced and the 'Create Journal Entry' button becomes enabled.

test_cases.test_case: Open Create Closure form

6. Click 'Create Closure'

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access Journal Entries page

At least one Office and relevant GL accounts configured

test_cases.test_case: Create Journal Entry blocked when required Transaction Date is left blank (date type)

At least one Office and relevant GL accounts configured
Journal Entries page is open
steps: 1. Click '+ Add Journal Entry' to open the Journal_Entry_Form

At least one Office and relevant GL accounts configured

test_cases.test_case: Create Journal Entry blocked when total debits do not equal total credits (balance constraint)
preconditions: Logged in with Accounting privileges
At least one Office and relevant GL accounts configured
Journal Entries page is open
steps: 1. Click '+ Add Journal Entry' to open the Journal_Entry_Form

test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked; an error is displayed indicating journal entries cannot be posted for dates on or before the existing closing date for the selected Office; the form does not submit and no journal entry is created

10. Click 'Create Journal Entry'
test_cases.expected_result: Create Journal Entry succeeds; a success confirmation is visible and the new journal entry appears in the Journal Entries table showing the entered Transaction Date and amounts

test_cases.test_case: Create journal entry where totals differ by one smallest currency unit (boundary fail)

11. Click 'Create Journal Entry'

In the new Definitions row: enter/select <loan product> for Loan Product, select 'SUB-STANDARD' for Category, enter <minimum overdue days> for Minimum Age, enter <maximum overdue days> for Maximum Age, enter <percentage> for Provisioning Percentage, select <liability account> and select <expense account>

test_cases.test_case: Edit Employee action unavailable when precondition (Office exists) is not met

test_cases.test_case: Create employee with very long First and Last Name (200+ chars)

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Create Employee to guard against duplicate creation

At least one Office exists in the system

4. Enter a valid Last Name
5. Click Create Employee

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user attempts to access Reports page

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated user without report-view permission tries to open a report's Parameters form

3. Click the report row's Name or the 'Open Parameters' link
test_cases.expected_result: Action is blocked; no Report_Parameters_Form opens; a visible authorization/error indicator is shown indicating the user lacks permission to view reports and the report row remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated user without Run Report permission attempts to run a report

2. Open a report's Report_Parameters_Form
3. Fill parameters with valid values (select Office, Branch, Currency, Loan Product, Fund, and a valid Date Range)
4. Click the 'Run Report' button
test_cases.expected_result: Run is blocked; form does not generate Report_Output_Table; a visible authorization/permission error is shown and the parameters form remains displayed

test_cases.test_case: Invalid Date_Range format prevents running the report
preconditions: User is authenticated with permission to view and run reports
Report_Parameters_Form is open
steps: 1. In the Report_Parameters_Form, enter <invalid date format> into the Date_Range field
2. Fill other parameters with valid values if required
3. Click the 'Run Report' button
test_cases.expected_result: Form does not submit; an inline validation error appears on the Date_Range field indicating the date is invalid/incorrect format; no Report_Output_Table is generated

test_cases.test_case: Run Report when system contains no relevant transactional or master data
preconditions: User is authenticated with permission to run reports
System contains no relevant transactional/master data for the selected parameters
steps: 1. Open a report's Report_Parameters_Form
2. Fill parameters with valid values (select Office, Branch, Currency, Loan Product, Fund, and a valid Date Range)
3. Click the 'Run Report' button
test_cases.expected_result: Report generation is blocked/produces no results; no Report_Output_Table with rows is displayed; a visible message or indicator informs the user that no data is available for the selected parameters and the parameters form or an empty result view remains visible

test_cases.test_case: Export actions are not available before a report is run (no Report_Output_Table present)
preconditions: User is authenticated with permission to view reports
No report has been run in the current session (no Report_Output_Table present)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Reports module and open a report's Report_Parameters_Form
2. Do not click 'Run Report'; observe the page where output would appear
3. Attempt to locate or click 'Export to Excel' or other export buttons
test_cases.expected_result: 'Export to Excel' (and other export actions) are not visible or are disabled when no Report_Output_Table exists; no file download is initiated and no export UI action occurs

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated user without export permission attempts to export generated report to Excel
preconditions: User is authenticated as <user_without_export_permission>
A Report_Output_Table with rows is present (a report has been run)
steps: 1. Log in as <user_without_export_permission>
2. Run a report to generate a Report_Output_Table with data
3. Click the 'Export to Excel' action
test_cases.expected_result: Export is blocked; no file download is initiated; a visible authorization/permission error is shown and the Report_Output_Table remains displayed

test_cases.test_case: Very long string entered into Office field
preconditions: User is authenticated with permission to view reports
Reports page is open and Report_Parameters_Form can be opened (report row available)
steps: 1. From Reports catalog, click 'Open Parameters' on any report row
2. In Report_Parameters_Form, focus the Office field
3. Enter a very long string (much greater than typical display length) into the Office field
4. Fill any other minimally required parameter fields
5. Click 'Run Report'
test_cases.expected_result: The Office field value is rejected and an inline error is shown next to the Office field indicating the value exceeds the allowed length; Run Report is blocked (report is not generated)

test_cases.test_case: Unicode and emoji characters entered into Loan_Product field
preconditions: User is authenticated with permission to view reports
Report_Parameters_Form is open for a selected report

3. Fill any other minimally required parameter fields

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of 'Run Report' to test duplicate submission handling

Report_Parameters_Form is open with all required parameters filled
steps: 1. Click 'Run Report' once
2. Immediately click 'Run Report' a second time (rapid double-click)
test_cases.expected_result: The second submission is blocked: the Run Report control becomes disabled or ignored and only one Report_Output_Table instance appears on screen (single result); no duplicate report execution UI is shown

test_cases.test_case: Export to Excel when report returns zero rows

4. Click 'Export to Excel' from the Report_Output_Table export actions
test_cases.expected_result: Export action succeeds: the browser begins downloading an Excel file (download visible in browser downloads); the UI remains on the Report_Output_Table screen showing the empty table

test_cases.test_case: Submit a valid one-time account transfer
preconditions: User logged in as <user with permissions for account transfers and standing instruction management>
Source and destination accounts exist with sufficient available balance
steps: 1. Navigate to the Account Transfers page

4. Select 'Savings Account' from the From Account Type dropdown
5. Select <From Account> in the From Account field
6. Select <To Office> in the To Office field
7. Select <To Client> in the To Client field
8. Select 'Savings Account' from the To Account Type dropdown
9. Select <To Account> in the To Account field
10. Enter <valid transfer amount not exceeding available balance> in the Transfer Amount field
11. Enter <Transfer Date> in the Transfer Date field
12. Enter <optional description> in the Description field
13. Click Submit
test_cases.expected_result: A success notification is displayed confirming the transfer; the Account Transfers page shows a confirmation summary listing the chosen From Account, To Account, the entered <Transfer Amount>, and <Transfer Date>

test_cases.test_case: Submit one-time transfer with amount exactly equal to available balance

Destination account exists

5. Enter a valid date in Transfer_Date

test_cases.test_case: Attempt transfer with amount one unit above available balance
preconditions: Authenticated user with permission to perform account transfers
Source account exists with available balance = <available balance>
Destination account exists
steps: 1. Navigate to the Account Transfers form

Source and destination accounts exist

10. Open the Tax Group selector dropdown

Holidays page is visible

steps: 1. On the Manage Scheduler Jobs table, click the row action 'Run Now' for <job name>
2. Observe the job row for immediate status changes
test_cases.expected_result: The table row for <job name> shows the job as currently running (the 'Currently Running' indicator is visible) or an immediate run status indicator is displayed in the row

test_cases.test_case: Open a job's Run History from the table
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Manage Scheduler Jobs page is open with <job name> listed
steps: 1. On the Manage Scheduler Jobs table, click the row action 'View Run History' for <job name>
test_cases.expected_result: The Job Run History page or panel opens for <job name>, showing recent runs with their statuses and timestamps

test_cases.test_case: Toggle a Global Configuration 'Enabled' flag inline
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Manage Global Configuration page is open with <configuration name> listed
steps: 1. Click the inline 'Enabled' checkbox for <configuration name> in the Global Configuration table
2. Wait for the row to reflect the change
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Enabled' checkbox for <configuration name> shows the updated state (checked or unchecked) in the table row

test_cases.test_case: Edit a Global Configuration entry via row 'Edit' action
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Manage Global Configuration page is open with <configuration name> listed
steps: 1. On the Global Configuration table, click the row action 'Edit' for <configuration name>
2. In the Edit dialog, change the Value to <valid value>
3. Click Save in the Edit dialog
test_cases.expected_result: The Global Configuration table row for <configuration name> displays the updated Value = <valid value>

test_cases.test_case: Update a Global Configuration Value via inline edit
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Manage Global Configuration page is open with <configuration name> listed
steps: 1. Click the Value cell for <configuration name> to enable inline editing
2. Enter <valid value> and confirm the inline edit
3. Wait for the table row to refresh
test_cases.expected_result: The Value cell for <configuration name> displays the updated <valid value> inline

test_cases.test_case: Open a code list to manage its values
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Manage Codes page is open with <code list name> listed
steps: 1. On the Manage Codes table, click the row action 'Open Code' for <code list name>
test_cases.expected_result: The Manage Code Values form opens for <code list name> displaying its existing values and controls to add, edit, reorder, and deactivate entries

test_cases.test_case: Add a new value to a code list using 'Add Value'
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Manage Code Values form is open for <code list name>
steps: 1. Click the 'Add Value' button within the Manage Code Values form
2. In the new value row, enter <value> into Value and optionally <code> into Code
3. Click 'Add Value' to submit
test_cases.expected_result: A new row appears in the code values list for <code list name> showing Value = <value> and Code = <code> (if provided)

test_cases.test_case: Persist edits, reorder and active toggles for code list values via 'Save Changes'
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Manage Code Values form is open for <code list name> with multiple values listed
steps: 1. Reorder the listed code values using the provided reorder controls
2. Toggle Is Active for at least one code value and edit the Value text for another value
3. Click 'Save Changes'
test_cases.expected_result: The code values list for <code list name> displays the new order, the updated Value text, and the changed Is Active checkbox states

test_cases.test_case: Open Create Data Table form in edit mode for an existing custom table
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Manage Data Tables page is open with <custom table name> listed
steps: 1. On the Manage Data Tables list, click the row action 'Edit' for <custom table name>
test_cases.expected_result: The Create Data Table form opens in edit mode populated with Data Table Name = <custom table name> and the table's existing column definitions listed

test_cases.test_case: Create a new custom data table with one column
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Manage Data Tables page is open
steps: 1. Click the control to create a new custom data table (opens Create Data Table form)
2. Enter <data table name> into Data Table Name
3. Select <application table> from Application Table Name dropdown
4. Check or leave unchecked the 'Multi Row' checkbox as required
5. In Columns, click 'Add Row' to add a column
6. For the new column row, enter <column name> into Column Name, select <type> from Type, optionally enter <length>, and set Is Mandatory and/or Is Unique as required
7. Click 'Create' to submit the form
test_cases.expected_result: The Manage Data Tables list displays a new row for <data table name> with Application Table = <application table> and the defined column visible in the table's details

test_cases.test_case: Cancel create custom data table and return to Manage Data Tables list
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Create Data Table form is open
steps: 1. Click the 'Cancel' button on the Create Data Table form
test_cases.expected_result: The Create Data Table form closes and the Manage Data Tables list is shown with no new table created

test_cases.test_case: Open audit entry details from the Audit Trails table
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
Audit Trails page is open with at least one audit entry listed
steps: 1. On the Audit Trails table, click the row action 'View Details' for an audit entry with Processing Result not necessarily Pending
test_cases.expected_result: An audit entry details panel or page opens showing the entry's Action Name, Entity Name, Resource ID, Maker, and timestamps

test_cases.test_case: Approve a pending command in Audit Trails when maker-checker is enabled
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
maker-checker (4-eyes approval) feature flag enabled
Audit Trails page is open and contains an audit row where Processing Result == Pending
steps: 1. In the Audit Trails table, locate the audit row with Processing Result = Pending for <resource identifier>
2. Click the row action 'Approve' for that pending audit row
3. Confirm any approval prompt if displayed
test_cases.expected_result: The Processing Result column for the selected audit row shows 'Approved' and the Approve action is no longer available for that row

test_cases.test_case: Reject a pending command with a reason in Audit Trails when maker-checker is enabled
preconditions: User logged in as <Administrator>
maker-checker (4-eyes approval) feature flag enabled
Audit Trails page is open and contains an audit row where Processing Result == Pending
steps: 1. In the Audit Trails table, locate the audit row with Processing Result = Pending for <resource identifier>
2. Click the row action 'Reject' for that pending audit row
3. In the Reject dialog, enter <reason> into the Reason field (required)
4. Click Confirm/Reject to submit the rejection
5. Open the audit entry details for the same row to verify recorded reason
test_cases.expected_result: The Processing Result column for the selected audit row shows 'Rejected' and the rejection reason entered is visible in the audit entry details

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access System Administration page
preconditions: Not logged in (no authenticated session)
steps: 1. In a new browser session with no authenticated user, navigate to the System Administration URL or click Admin > System Administration
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Login page; System Administration content (scheduler table, global configuration table, audit trails) is not displayed and no admin controls are accessible

test_cases.test_case: Authenticated non-admin user is blocked from System Administration controls
preconditions: Logged in as a user WITHOUT administrative privileges
steps: 1. Log in as a user account that does not have administrative privileges
2. Attempt to open Admin > System Administration
test_cases.expected_result: Access to System Administration is blocked: the Admin System Administration content is not displayed and admin controls (Start/Stop Scheduler toggle, job row action buttons, global configuration toggles) are not present; the page displays an authorization/permission indicator and the user remains on a non-admin view

test_cases.test_case: Approve button is not available for an audit entry whose Processing Result is not Pending (Approved state)
preconditions: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges
An audit entry exists with Processing Result == Approved
steps: 1. Log in as an administrator
2. Navigate to Admin > Audit Trails
3. Locate and open the audit entry whose Processing Result is <Approved>
4. Attempt to click an Approve action for that entry
test_cases.expected_result: The Approve action is not available for the Approved entry (Approve button is not visible or is disabled); attempting the action does not change the entry — Processing Result remains <Approved> and no approval occurs

test_cases.test_case: Approve/Reject actions are not available when maker-checker (4-eyes) feature flag is disabled
preconditions: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges
maker-checker (4-eyes approval) feature flag is currently <disabled>
steps: 1. Log in as an administrator
2. Navigate to Admin > Global Configuration
3. Ensure the maker-checker feature flag is set to <disabled> (toggle if necessary)
4. Navigate to Admin > Audit Trails
5. Open an audit entry whose Processing Result is <Pending>
6. Observe available row actions for the pending entry
test_cases.expected_result: Approve and Reject actions are not shown for the Pending audit entry while maker-checker is disabled; the pending command cannot be approved or rejected from the UI and Processing Result remains <Pending>

test_cases.test_case: Reject action blocked when required Reason field is left blank
preconditions: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges
An audit entry exists with Processing Result == Pending
steps: 1. Log in as an administrator
2. Navigate to Admin > Audit Trails
3. Open an audit entry whose Processing Result is <Pending>
4. Click the Reject row action for that entry
5. Leave the Reason field blank
6. Click the Submit/Reject button in the Reject dialog
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Reason field indicating it is required; the Reject action is blocked; the audit entry's Processing Result remains <Pending> (no rejection occurs)

test_cases.test_case: Approve action unavailable for an audit entry in Rejected state
preconditions: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges
An audit entry exists with Processing Result == Rejected
steps: 1. Log in as an administrator
2. Navigate to Admin > Audit Trails
3. Locate and open the audit entry whose Processing Result is <Rejected>
4. Attempt to click an Approve action for that entry
test_cases.expected_result: The Approve action is not available for the Rejected entry (Approve button not visible or disabled); attempting approval does not change the entry — Processing Result remains <Rejected>

test_cases.test_case: Invalid date entered into Audit Trails Date Range filter is rejected
preconditions: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges
steps: 1. Log in as an administrator
2. Navigate to Admin > Audit Trails
3. Enter <invalid date format or impossible date> into the Date Range filter field
4. Click the Search / Apply Filters button
test_cases.expected_result: Date Range field displays an inline validation error indicating the date is invalid; the filter is not applied and the audit results remain unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Run Now' while global scheduler is stopped
preconditions: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges and viewing the Admin/System Administration section
At least one job is present in the Manage Scheduler Jobs table
steps: 1. Click the global 'Start/Stop Scheduler' toggle to set Scheduler_Enabled = Off
2. In the Manage Scheduler Jobs table, locate a job row
3. Click the row action 'Run Now' for that job
test_cases.expected_result: Queuing the job is blocked; a visible inline or modal error is shown indicating the scheduler is disabled and the job was not queued; the Manage Scheduler Jobs table shows no change to the job's 'Currently Running' indicator

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to deactivate a job that shows 'Currently Running'
preconditions: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges and viewing the Admin/System Administration section
At least one job row in Manage Scheduler Jobs currently displays the 'Currently Running' indicator = true and Is_Active = true
steps: 1. In the job row that is currently running, click the 'Is Active' toggle to set it Off
test_cases.expected_result: The toggle action is blocked; an inline error is shown indicating the job cannot be deactivated while it is currently running; the Is Active checkbox remains checked in the UI

test_cases.test_case: Inline edit CRON expression containing leading and trailing whitespace is persisted trimmed
preconditions: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges and viewing the Admin/System Administration section
Manage Scheduler Jobs table contains at least one editable job
steps: 1. In a job row, click the inline CRON expression cell or the 'Edit CRON' action to open the editor
2. Enter a CRON expression value that has leading and trailing whitespace surrounding the valid expression
3. Click Save (persist the edited CRON expression)
test_cases.expected_result: Save succeeds; the job row displays the persisted CRON Expression with leading and trailing whitespace removed (trimmed) and the editor is closed

test_cases.test_case: Apply Audit Trails Date Range filter where Start Date = End Date (same-day)
preconditions: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges and viewing the Admin/System Administration section
Audit Trails page is open
steps: 1. Open the Date Range filter controls on the Audit Trails page
2. Set the Start Date to a particular day
3. Set the End Date to the same day as the Start Date
4. Click Apply Filters
test_cases.expected_result: Filter applies successfully; the audit table shows entries for that single date and the Date Range filter UI continues to show the selected same-day range

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to apply Audit Trails Date Range where Start Date is after End Date
preconditions: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges and viewing the Admin/System Administration section
Audit Trails page is open
steps: 1. Open the Date Range filter controls on the Audit Trails page
2. Set the Start Date to a day that is one day after the End Date
3. Set the End Date to a day that is one day before the Start Date
4. Click Apply Filters
test_cases.expected_result: Applying the filter is blocked; an inline validation error is shown indicating the start date must be on or before the end date and the audit table contents remain unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Add a code list value with a very long Value and special characters in Code
preconditions: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges and viewing the Admin/System Administration section
Manage Codes page is open and at least one code list is present
steps: 1. Click a code list row to open its Manage Code Values screen
2. Click 'Add Value' to create a new value row
3. In the new row, enter a Value consisting of a very long string (200+ characters) into the Value field
4. Enter a Code value containing special characters and punctuation into the Code field
5. Click 'Add Value' (or 'Save Changes' if required to persist)
test_cases.expected_result: The new value row is added to the values list and displays the entered Value and Code as entered (no silent truncation); the new row appears in the list with its Is Active state visible

test_cases.test_case: Open Profile Menu reveals logout option
preconditions: User is currently authenticated with an active session and a valid authentication token.
steps: 1. Click the User Profile icon in the top-right navigation bar.
test_cases.expected_result: The User Profile dropdown is visible showing the 'Profile Settings' link and the 'Log Out' button.

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to Profile Settings from profile menu
preconditions: User is currently authenticated with an active session and a valid authentication token.
steps: 1. Click the User Profile icon to open the profile menu.
2. Click the 'Profile Settings' entry in the dropdown.
test_cases.expected_result: The Profile Settings page is displayed (the Profile Settings page content and heading are visible).

test_cases.test_case: Log out terminates session and returns to Login page
preconditions: User is currently authenticated with an active session and a valid authentication token.
steps: 1. Click the User Profile icon to open the profile menu.
2. Click the 'Log Out' button in the dropdown.
test_cases.expected_result: The Login page is displayed; the user profile icon is no longer present in the top navigation, indicating the user is logged out.

test_cases.test_case: After logout, attempting to access an authenticated route redirects to Login
preconditions: User has just logged out and is on the Login page.
steps: 1. In the browser address bar, navigate to an authenticated URL by entering <authenticated route> and pressing Enter.
test_cases.expected_result: The Login page is displayed; the requested authenticated page is not shown (the attempt to open <authenticated route> redirects to the Login page).

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot reach Log Out (precondition not met)
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no active session / not logged in)
steps: 1. Open the application landing URL as an unauthenticated user
2. Observe the top-right corner of the persistent top navigation bar
test_cases.expected_result: The User Profile icon is not visible in the top-right corner and the Log Out option is not available; the Log Out action cannot be invoked from the UI (no Log Out control present) and the Login page / Login form is displayed

test_cases.test_case: After logging out, navigating to Profile Settings is blocked and redirects to Login
preconditions: User is authenticated with an active session
steps: 1. Click the User Profile icon in the top-right to reveal the profile dropdown
2. Click the Log Out button in the profile dropdown
3. In the browser address bar, attempt to navigate to the Profile Settings page by entering <Profile Settings page URL> and pressing Enter
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to the Profile Settings page is blocked: the application redirects to the Login page instead of showing Profile Settings; Profile Settings content is not displayed and no User Profile icon is visible (user remains unauthenticated)

test_cases.test_case: Direct access to the Log Out action while unauthenticated is rejected / redirected
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no active session)
steps: 1. Open the browser and enter <Log Out action URL> (direct logout endpoint or bookmarked logout link) as an unauthenticated user
2. Press Enter to load the URL
test_cases.expected_result: The request is rejected/handled as unauthenticated: the Login page is displayed instead of any authenticated page; no logout processing of an active session occurs (there was no active session) and no authenticated UI (User Profile icon) is shown

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click Log Out from profile dropdown
preconditions: User is authenticated and sees User_Profile_Icon in the top navigation
steps: 1. Click User_Profile_Icon to open User_Profile_Dropdown
2. Click Log_Out
3. Immediately click Log_Out a second time (second click occurs before redirect completes)
test_cases.expected_result: Log out succeeds: the Login page is displayed and the user is not returned to any authenticated page; the second click does not produce a visible error or create a second active session.

test_cases.test_case: Press browser Back after successful logout
preconditions: User is authenticated and sees User_Profile_Icon in the top navigation
steps: 1. Click User_Profile_Icon to open User_Profile_Dropdown
2. Click Log_Out
3. After being redirected, click the browser Back button
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked: after pressing Back the Login page is shown (user is not returned to authenticated content and authenticated pages redirect to Login).

test_cases.test_case: Manually enter an authenticated-route URL immediately after logout
preconditions: User is authenticated and sees User_Profile_Icon in the top navigation
steps: 1. Click User_Profile_Icon to open User_Profile_Dropdown
2. Click Log_Out
3. In the browser address bar, enter the URL of a known authenticated page and press Enter
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: user is redirected to and shown the Login page instead of the requested authenticated page.

test_cases.test_case: Click Profile Settings after initiating Log Out from the open dropdown
preconditions: User is authenticated and sees User_Profile_Icon in the top navigation
steps: 1. Click User_Profile_Icon to open User_Profile_Dropdown
2. Click Log_Out
3. Immediately click Profile_Settings link in the same dropdown (second click after logout was initiated)
test_cases.expected_result: Log out succeeds and navigation to Profile Settings is blocked: the Login page is displayed and the Profile Settings page is not shown.

module_context.summary: Provides the authentication interface for users to sign into Mifos with tenant, username, and password, handling validation and error feedback.
module_context.where_it_fits: This is the application's entry point before accessing the top navigation (Institution, Accounting, Reports, Admin, etc.) and the left sidebar shortcuts; successful authentication redirects users into the Dashboard and the main application areas described in the navigation overview.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Unauthenticated user session presenting the login screen with Tenant, Username, and Password available; network connectivity to the authentication backend.

module_context.summary: The Home Page presents a welcome card, recent activity search and quick access to the Dashboard, along with system version information.
module_context.where_it_fits: Displayed immediately after successful login as the application's landing view, accessible via the top navigation bar context (e.g., after selecting Institution or from the persistent top bar) and serving as the launch point to the Dashboard and other sections reachable through the left sidebar and top navigation menus.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user session (successful login) with application context loaded

module_context.summary: Overview page presenting client growth trends and high-level financial summary cards for a selected office.
module_context.where_it_fits: This is an overview module accessed from the Home Page via the Dashboard button and appears in the main content area beneath the top navigation and breadcrumbs; it provides front‑line metrics (charts and summary cards) used before drilling into Clients, Accounts, or Reports.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user on the Home Page with an office context available (selected or default)

module_context.summary: Provides a global, case-insensitive search across Clients, Groups, Loans, and Savings accounts with grouped, partial-match results that link to entity detail pages.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation bar's search icon and available from any page after login, it serves as a session-wide navigation and discovery tool alongside the left sidebar shortcuts (Clients, Groups, Centers, Products) and breadcrumb navigation. It operates across Institution-related modules to quickly surface entities and jump to their detail pages.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with access to the application (and permission to view Clients/Groups/Loans/Savings) and is on any in-app page.

module_context.summary: Displays and manages the application’s clients via a searchable/filterable list, multi-step create wizard, bulk import, and detailed client lifecycle and account actions.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located under Institution > Clients in the top navigation and reachable via the Clients icon in the left sidebar, this module is the primary client administration area within the application where users view client listings, import/create clients, and manage client status and related accounts as part of routine operations.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with permission to access the Institution > Clients section (or Clients sidebar shortcut).

module_context.summary: Provides listing, creation, bulk import, and detailed management of client groups including membership, group loans/savings, meetings, and group-level actions.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located under Institution → Groups and reachable via the left-sidebar Groups icon, this module sits within the Client/Group Management area of the application and supports group lifecycle and operations (onboarding, meetings, loans/savings linkage, and administration).
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with permissions to view/manage groups and an active Institution/Office context; relevant client records exist for association when creating groups.

module_context.summary: Management interface for creating, importing, viewing, and administering Centers—organizational units that contain Groups—with detail pages, action buttons, membership tabs, and collection-sheet generation.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the Institution menu (Institution > Centers) and via the left sidebar Centers icon, this module lives among core client-organization management screens and is used after authentication when managing field-office structure and group aggregates; it links to Group detail pages and to account-related tabs (Loan/Savings) within the center lifecycle. It integrates with list and detail patterns used across the app (Material tables for listings, card layouts for details, and form-based create/import workflows).
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with access to Institution features and at least one Office exists (or selected) to associate with a Center; relevant Groups may already exist for addition.

module_context.summary: Lists existing loan products and provides a 6-step wizard for creating or editing detailed loan product configurations including terms, currency, charges, and accounting mappings.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the application's left sidebar Products shortcut and via the Institution area in the top navigation, the Loan Products page appears as an entity listing (with breadcrumbs) where administrators define product templates before loan accounts are created and managed elsewhere in the lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with permission to view and manage product configuration (access to Products/Institution sections) and at least one Fund and any referenced Charges/GL accounts preconfigured if accounting mappings will be used.

module_context.summary: Manages creation, configuration, and listing of savings, fixed deposit, and recurring deposit product definitions via a multi-step wizard and searchable product table.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located under the Products section (accessible from the left sidebar shortcuts and within the Institution/Product area), this module is part of product lifecycle configuration used by administrators to define interest, fees, accounting mappings, and operational rules before deposit accounts are opened from the main account workflows. Breadcrumbs and Material Design tables situate it among other product and accounting configuration pages.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with product-management permissions and has navigated to the Products area (Savings Products page) in the application; no specific client or account is required.

module_context.summary: Manages the catalog of share product definitions, including listing existing share products and a 7-step wizard for creating or editing product configurations and accounting mappings.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located under the Products area accessible from the left sidebar (and reachable from the Institution/Product navigation), this module is an entity listing and detail workflow used during product setup and maintenance prior to creating share accounts or issuing shares. It sits alongside other product modules (Loan/Savings/Share Products) and ties into accounting and charges configuration in the application lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with product-management privileges and access to the Products section; the application context is on the Share Products listing page.

module_context.summary: Manages fee and penalty definitions (charges) including creation, viewing, editing, and deletion of charge rules with attributes like applies-to, timing, calculation, amount, tax group, and payment mode.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is part of the product/financial configuration area of the application and is reachable from the left sidebar Products shortcut and related configuration sections in the top navigation (e.g., Institution/Accounting or Configuration areas); it is used when defining fees and penalties that apply to loans, savings, shares, and clients across the platform.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with an active organization selected and has permissions to manage product/charge definitions (admin/configuration role).

module_context.summary: Defines and maintains floating interest rate schedules and their histories that automatically adjust linked loan product interest rates.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located within the Products area reachable from the left sidebar shortcuts or via the Institution/Product navigation paths, this module is an entity listing/detail page in the main content area used during loan product configuration and rate management; it integrates with Loan Products so rate periods affect loan interest calculations. Breadcrumbs and Material table/listing conventions are used to navigate to and within this module.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with permissions to view/manage product or configuration settings and the application is at the Products/Floatings Rates listing view

module_context.summary: Manages definitions of delinquency ranges and buckets that classify overdue loans for reporting, provisioning, and collection workflows.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located in the administrative/configuration area tied to product settings and portfolio reporting—reachable via the application's navigation where Products appear in the left sidebar and administrative pages are accessible from the top Institution menu (e.g., Institution → Admin or Reports); Delinquency buckets are associated with Loan Products and used during loan lifecycle processing.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with configuration/admin privileges and access to Loan Products (at least one loan product exists to which buckets can be linked).

module_context.summary: Manages creation, approval, disbursement, lifecycle actions, and detailed views of individual loan accounts including repayment schedules, transactions, charges, collateral, and documents.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the Institution > Clients flow and via the Clients shortcut in the left sidebar, the Loan Account module is reached when initiating a new loan from a Client Detail page or when opening an existing loan from client listings; it sits in the transaction lifecycle between loan product configuration (Loan Products) and accounting/repayment management (Transactions, Accounting).
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with loan-management permissions and is either viewing a specific Client's Detail page (to start a new application) or selecting an existing Loan Account for inspection or action.

module_context.summary: Manages creation, lifecycle and transaction handling for client savings accounts, including balances, status transitions, charges, and interest operations.
module_context.where_it_fits: Reached from a Client's Detail page (accessible via Institution → Clients in the top nav or the Clients shortcut in the left sidebar) and displayed in the main content area beneath breadcrumbs; it links account operations (deposits, withdrawals, interest posting, status changes) into the broader accounting and reporting areas of the application.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in user with permissions to manage accounts, viewing a specific Client Detail page, and at least one Savings Product configured/available for selection.

module_context.summary: Manages creation, lifecycle transitions, and detailed viewing of client share investment accounts including applications, approvals, activations, purchases, redemptions, dividends, and associated charges.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the Client Detail page under Institution → Clients (also reachable via the Clients icon in the left sidebar), the Share Account module appears as a detail and form workflow in the client management area and integrates into the account lifecycle and accounting/reporting flows shown in the breadcrumbs and main content area.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with permission to manage client accounts and is viewing a specific Client Detail page; at least one Share Product exists and the client may have active savings accounts for fee/deposit linkage.

module_context.summary: Manages creation, lifecycle actions, and detailed views for Fixed Deposit (FD) and Recurring Deposit (RD) accounts including schedules, balances, interest calculations, and maturity operations.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessed from a Client Detail page within the Institution → Clients flow (and reachable via product/account shortcuts in the left sidebar), this module is part of the Products/Accounts area showing FD and RD accounts for individual clients. It integrates with product configuration (deposit products and interest rate charts) and appears alongside other account detail pages with tabs for Summary, Transactions, and Charges.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: An active client is selected on the Client Detail page and relevant FD/RD products and interest rate charts are configured in the system.

module_context.summary: Displays a hierarchical tree of all General Ledger accounts by type with attributes, creation and detail/edit capabilities for GL accounts.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is part of the top-level Accounting area (accessible via the top navigation bar: Accounting → Chart of Accounts) and is used during chart setup and maintenance before or alongside journal entry and accounting operations. It links into the broader accounting workflow that includes Journal Entries & Closures and Accounting Rules.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with a role that has Accounting access and an organization instance configured; Accounting module accessible.

module_context.summary: Manages general ledger journal entries and period closures by displaying, creating, and validating journal entries and recording accounting period closings.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located under the top navigation Accounting menu and reachable via the left sidebar Accounting shortcuts, this module is part of the core Accounting section that operates on ledger data; it is used during regular accounting workflows to record transactions and to finalize periods by creating closures that lock prior dates against further postings.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in with Accounting privileges, at least one Office and relevant GL accounts configured, and access to the Accounting section of the application.

module_context.summary: Manages accounting rules that define debit/credit GL account behavior and maps system financial activities to specific General Ledger (GL) accounts for automated posting.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located within the application's Accounting area (accessible from the top navigation bar under Accounting or via Institution → Accounting), this module sits on the configuration side of the accounting lifecycle to control how transactions and system-level financial events are posted to the ledger. It is used alongside chart-of-accounts and journal entry features to ensure automated and consistent accounting postings.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with accounting/configuration privileges and the organization's chart of GL accounts and Offices are already configured.

module_context.summary: Manages provisioning criteria for categorizing overdue loans and generates provisioning journal entries that record calculated provisions per loan product and category.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located within the Accounting area of the application (accessible from the top navigation Accounting menu and relevant accounting pages in the left sidebar), this module provides both the configuration of provisioning rules and the operational generation/review of provisioning journal entries as part of month-end or provisioning workflows tied to the loan portfolio lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in with accounting/administrative privileges, a populated Chart of Accounts (GL accounts) exists, and the loan portfolio (including overdue loans) is present in the system.

module_context.summary: Manages and displays the hierarchical branch/office structure, including listing, creation, and detail/edit of individual offices and their metadata.
module_context.where_it_fits: Presented in the main content area and reachable from the top Institution menu (Institution > Offices) and the left sidebar organizational shortcuts; it sits early in organizational configuration and underpins client, staff, and accounting entities throughout the application lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with permissions to view/manage offices and an existing Head Office configured as the root of the hierarchy.

module_context.summary: Management interface for creating, listing, and editing employee (staff) records including office, loan officer assignment, contact details, and active status.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the application's navigation (via the top bar under Institution -> Admin and the left sidebar Employees shortcut), this module is part of institution/staff administration and supports operational workflows by supplying staff and loan officer assignments used across clients, groups, centers, and loan creation.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with staff-management permissions and at least one Office exists in the system.

module_context.summary: Manages tellers and their cashier assignments, including creation, allocation, cash operations (allocate/settle), and transaction history in a tabular/detail view.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is part of the application's operational/administration functionality and is surfaced as an entity listing and detail area in the main content region (accessible from the top navigation's Institution area and other admin sections), using the same breadcrumbed layout and Material Design tables/forms as other entity pages.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with teller/cashier management permissions, and at least one Office and at least one Staff record exist in the system.

module_context.summary: Management interface for creating and administering application users and role-based permissions, including user accounts, password policies, and a permissions matrix for roles.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located under the top navigation Admin section (accessible from the Institution/Admin area) and surfaced via breadcrumbs in the main content area, this module is an administrative configuration area used to define who can access which menus, pages, and actions across the application lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in with administrative privileges (permission to manage users/roles); Offices and Staff records exist if creating or linking users.

module_context.summary: Displays a catalog of grouped financial and operational reports and allows generation of report outputs with parameterized filters and export options.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the application's top navigation under Reports (and via Institution → Reports), this module sits in the reporting/analytics area of the platform and is used after transactional data has been recorded to generate tables, charts, and exportable files for monitoring and analysis.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated with permission to view reports and the system contains relevant transactional and master data (offices, products, loan/savings accounts, funds) for report parameters.

module_context.summary: Handles one-time account-to-account transfers and management of recurring standing instructions that move funds between client accounts.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the Institution menu and via account-related shortcuts in the left sidebar, this module appears in the core transaction flow where users view account details (breadcrumbs reflect the navigation path) and need to move funds or configure automatic transfers; it complements account and client management pages by executing debits/credits and scheduling recurring transfers.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with permissions for account transfers and standing instruction management, and existing source and destination accounts (with sufficient available balance for a one-time transfer).

module_context.summary: Configuration and management of tax components and tax groups that map tax rates to GL accounts and that can be applied to products and charge definitions to automatically calculate and post tax journal entries.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located within the platform's administrative/accounting configuration area (accessible from the top navigation under Accounting or Admin and related from Products via the left sidebar), this module is part of the product and accounting setup lifecycle and is referenced when creating or editing savings products and charge definitions. It appears as entity listing and detail pages (breadcrumbs shown in the content area) consistent with the app's Material Design tables and forms.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in with Accounting/Admin privileges and an existing Chart of Accounts (GL accounts) configured; relevant product or charge definitions exist when intending to reference a Tax Group.

module_context.summary: A centralized administration module for configuring organization-wide settings such as holidays, working days, currencies, funds, payment types, and bulk data import.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located under the top navigation Admin menu as the Organization section (visible via Admin > Organization Settings and reflected in breadcrumbs in the content area); it is part of the application's administrative configuration workflow used during initial setup and ongoing institutional maintenance.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user with administrative privileges (access to the Admin menu) and an existing organization context configured in the application.

module_context.summary: System Administration provides centralized management of scheduled jobs, global feature flags, code lists, custom data tables, and audit trails for the application.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is an administrative area exposed via the top navigation (Admin) and appears in the content area with breadcrumbs and icon-based sidebar context; it is used for system-wide configuration and background job lifecycle management across the application. It sits at the platform-level configuration layer, affecting Clients, Accounts, Products and reporting behaviors referenced elsewhere in the app.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a user with administrative privileges (role permitting access to Admin/System Administration) and viewing the Admin section in the top navigation.

module_context.summary: Ends the current authenticated user session by clearing authentication credentials and returning the application to the login screen.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the user profile dropdown in the top-right of the persistent top navigation bar (near global search, language selection, notifications), this function is part of session lifecycle management and is reachable from any page across the app via the profile icon in the top navigation. After invocation it redirects to the Login page and prevents access to authenticated routes until re-authentication.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is currently authenticated with an active session and a valid authentication token.
